mnemonics: FIRST Rules: Forward chaining, Inference, Rule-Based Systems, Symbolic representation
keyPoints: Rules use IF-THEN statements to represent knowledge and make inferences.
Forward chaining is data-driven, starting from known facts to reach a conclusion.
Backward chaining is goal-driven, starting from a hypothesis and working backward to prove it.
commonMistakes: Confusing forward chaining (data-driven) with backward chaining (goal-driven).
Creating rules with overly broad conditions that lead to incorrect inferences.
Forgetting that rule-based systems can struggle with uncertainty or incomplete knowledge.
